ProductType.FullText: The product is intended for research, analysis and scientific education.

ClpHazardStatement.FullText: May cause an allergic skin reaction.
May damage fertility. May damage the unborn child. (...)
May cause an allergic skin reaction.
May damage fertility. May damage the unborn child. (-)

ClpPrecautionaryStatement.FullText: Do not handle until all safety precautions have been read and understood.
Avoid contact during pregnancy and while nursing.
Contaminated work clothing should not be allowed out of the workplace.
Wear protective gloves/protective clothing and eye protection/face protection.
IF exposed or concerned: Get medical advice/attention.
If skin irritation or rash occurs: Get medical advice/attention.
Take off contaminated clothing and wash it before reuse.

HealthEffect.FullText: The mixture contains >= 0.1% of substances that have endocrine disrupting properties. See SECTION 3 of this safety data sheet.

EnvironmentalEffect.FullText: The mixture contains >= 0.1 % substances meeting the PBT criteria according to Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, Annex XIII. See SECTION 3 in this safety data sheet.
The mixture contains >= 0.1 % substances meeting the vPvB criteria according to Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, Annex XIII. See SECTION 3 in this safety data sheet.

ClpHazardStatement.FullText: May damage fertility. May damage the unborn child. (...)

Species.FullText: Raphidocelis subcapitata (previous names: Pseudokirchneriella subcapitata, Selenastrum capricornutum)
Raphidocelis subcapitata (previous names: Pseudokirchneriella subcapitata, Selenastrum capricornutum)

Method.FullText: OECD Guideline 202 (Daphnia sp. Acute Immobilisation Test)

ClpHazardStatement.FullText: Causes severe skin burns and eye damage.
May cause an allergic skin reaction.
Very toxic to aquatic life with long lasting effects.

Species.FullText: Raphidocelis subcapitata (previous names: Pseudokirchneriella subcapitata, Selenastrum capricornutum)
Raphidocelis subcapitata (previous names: Pseudokirchneriella subcapitata, Selenastrum capricornutum)
Raphidocelis subcapitata (previous names: Pseudokirchneriella subcapitata, Selenastrum capricornutum)

GeneralInformation.FullText: In case of accident or unwellness, seek medical advice immediately (show directions for use or safety data sheet if possible).
Remove victim out of the danger area.
If unconscious but breathing normally, place in recovery position and seek medical advice.
Do not leave affected person unattended.
First aider: Pay attention to self-protection!

FirstAidInhalation.FullText: In case of respiratory tract irritation, consult a physician.
Get medical advice/attention if you feel unwell.

FirstAidSkin.FullText: After contact with skin, wash immediately with plenty of water and soap.
Take off immediately all contaminated clothing.
If skin irritation or rash occurs: Get medical advice/attention.

FirstAidEye.FullText: In case of contact with eyes flush immediately with plenty of flowing water for 10 to 15 minutes holding eyelids apart and consult an ophthalmologist.
Remove contact lenses, if present and easy to do. Continue rinsing.

FirstAidIngestion.FullText: Let 1 glass of water be drunken in little sips (dilution effect).
Get medical advice/attention if you feel unwell.

PersonalProtectionFirstAider.FullText: No direct artificial respiration to be given by first aider.

SymptomsAndEffectsGeneral.FullText: May damage fertility or the unborn child.

MediaToBeUsed.FullText: Co-ordinate fire-fighting measures to the fire surroundings.

FireAndExplosionHazards.FullText: Collect contaminated fire extinguishing water separately. Do not allow entering drains or surface water.

SpecialProtectiveEquipmentForFirefighter.FullText: Fight fire with normal precautions from a reasonable distance.
In case of fire: Wear self-contained breathing apparatus.

FireAndExplosionComments.FullText: Do not inhale explosion and combustion gases.

PersonalPrecautions.FullText: Use personal protective equipment as required.
Avoid contact with skin, eyes and clothes.

ProtectiveEquipment.FullText: Wear protective gloves/protective clothing and eye protection/face protection.

EnvironmentalPrecautions.FullText: Do not allow to enter into surface water or drains.

Containment.FullText: Absorb with liquid-binding material (sand, diatomaceous earth, acid- or universal binding agents).

AdditionalInformation.FullText: Use appropriate container to avoid environmental contamination.

HandlingPrecautions.FullText: Wear personal protection equipment (refer to section 8).
Wash hands before breaks and after work.
Avoid exposure - obtain special instructions before use.
Persons with a history of asthma, allergies, chronic or recurrent respiratory disease should not be exposed to any process in which this product is used.

GeneralOccupationalHygiene.FullText: Avoid contact with skin, eyes and clothes.
When using do not eat, drink, smoke, sniff.

TechnicalMeasuresAndStorageConditions.FullText: Keep container tightly closed in a cool, well-ventilated place.

RequirementsForStorageRoomsAndVessels.FullText: Keep container tightly closed and in a well-ventilated place.

SuitableRespiratoryProtectionEquipment.FullText: Observe the wear time limits according GefStoffV in combination with the rules for using respiratory protection apparatus (BGR 190).

SuitableProtectiveClothing.FullText: Tested protective gloves must be worn
Take recovery periods for skin regeneration.
In the case of wanting to use the gloves again, clean them before taking off and air them well.
Breakthrough times and swelling properties of the material must be taken into consideration.

OrganisationalMeasures.FullText: Do not allow to enter into surface water or drains.

ReactivityDescription.FullText: The product itself does not burn.

StabilityDescription.FullText: The product is chemically stable under recommended conditions of storage, use and temperature.

HazardousReactions.FullText: No hazardous reaction when handled and stored according to provisions.

ConditionsToAvoid.FullText: Further information on proper storage: see section 7.

AcuteToxicityHumanExperience.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.
Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.
Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

Irritation.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

EyeIrritation.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

RespiratoryOrSkinSensitisationGeneral.FullText: May cause an allergic skin reaction.

AssessmentGermCellMutagenicityClassifica.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

AssessmentCarcinogenicityClassification.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

ReproductiveToxicityOtherInfo.FullText: May damage fertility. May damage the unborn child.

SpecificTargetOrganSEHumanExperience.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

SpecificTargetOrganREHumanExperience.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

AssessmentAspirationHazardClassification.FullText: Based on available data, the classification criteria are not met.

ProductWaste.FullText: Consult the appropriate local waste disposal expert about waste disposal.

PackagingWaste.FullText: Handle contaminated packages in the same way as the substance itself.

EuWasteRegulations.FullText: Dispose of waste according to applicable legislation.
